seo.title: LTX 2 Fast | AI Video Model, Specs & Pricing | MaxVideoAI
seo.description: Use the supported LTX 2 Fast model on MaxVideoAI for quick audio-ready drafts, then move to LTX 2.3 Fast for the current fast LTX workflow.

overview: LTX 2 Fast remains available on MaxVideoAI as a supported older fast LTX model for quick audio-ready drafts and legacy fast workflows.

hero.title: LTX 2 Fast — supported older LTX model for fast audio-ready drafts
hero.intro: LTX 2 Fast remains available for quick audio-ready drafts and older fast workflows, while LTX 2.3 Fast is now the main current fast LTX option.

secondaryLinks.label: Compare LTX 2.3 Fast vs LTX 2.3 Pro

technicalOverview.label: Inputs
technicalOverview.body: Text → Video; Image → Video (PNG/JPG/WebP, ~25 MB), prompt + seed/negatives

technicalOverview.label: Audio
technicalOverview.body: Native audio on by default; toggle off for silent runs

promptStructure.title: Prompting LTX 2 Fast
promptStructure.quote: Cinematic [subject] in [environment], [camera move], [lighting/style], [duration 6–20s], 16:9, audio on with [ambience/dialogue/music].
promptStructure.description: Keep it concise: subject + environment + motion + style + duration + audio cue.
steps: Camera move and pacing (fast, slow, tracking, dolly)

tips.title: Tips & limits
items: Use 50 fps for crisper motion on 6–10s runs.
Keep one clear action; use seeds/negative prompts to refine.
Audio is on by default—toggle off for cheaper silent drafts.

prompts.title: T2V cinematic test
prompts.prompt: A cowboy walking through a dusty western town at high noon, tracking shot from behind, cinematic depth, realistic lighting, 4K film grain, native ambience.

prompts.title: I2V neon portrait
prompts.prompt: A woman stands still amid a busy neon-lit street at night. The camera slowly dollies in toward her face as people blur past, their motion emphasizing her calm presence. City lights flicker and reflections shift across her denim jacket.
prompts.notes: Set duration 6–10s or 12–20s depending on pace; keep 16:9.

prompts.title: Example prompt – 8s city crosswalk
prompts.prompt: A young woman crosses a busy city street at golden hour, camera tracking her from a low side angle as she steps onto the crosswalk and walks toward the opposite sidewalk. Warm sunlight hits the side of her face, gentle lens flares appear on the edges of the frame, and cars roll past in the background. Audio: soft city ambience (cars, footsteps, distant voices) plus a light upbeat music bed, no dialogue, no on-screen text.

prompts.title: Example prompt – 18–20s office walkthrough
prompts.prompt: One continuous 18–20 second 16:9 shot inside a bright startup office during the day. The camera starts at the entrance as the glass door opens, then walks forward in one smooth motion behind a team lead. We glide past desks with people working on laptops, a whiteboard covered in sketches, and a lounge corner where two colleagues laugh over a prototype. The camera never cuts and finally settles into a medium shot of the team lead stopping by a large window, turning to camera with a relaxed smile. Audio: natural office ambience (typing, low chatter, distant coffee machine) with a gentle motivational music track underneath, no dialogue, no on-screen text.

faqs.q: How long can LTX 2 Fast run?
faqs.a: 6–20 seconds. Durations above 10 seconds must run at 1080p and 25 fps per Fal constraints.
Yes, native audio is on by default. You can toggle it off for silent exports.
16:9 only in the current Fast routing. Use Wan or Pika for flexible ratios.

custom.heroDesc1: Create 6–20 second clips with native audio, 1080p–4K and 25/50 fps in one card. LTX 2 Fast is the “instant” tier of the LTX 2 family: perfect for rapid social cuts, drafts, storyboards and mood pieces when you want 4K quality without waiting.
custom.heroDesc2: Above 10 seconds we automatically keep runs at 1080p/25 fps to match the Fal routing and keep turnarounds reliable.
custom.heroSupportLine: Use it for hooks, pacing, and mood exploration, then regenerate winners in the premium tier.
heroHighlights: Built for iteration speed||Make lots of variations fast without overthinking the first pass.
More room per clip||Better for pacing, build-up, and “mini-scene” beats than ultra-short engines.
Sound-ready drafts||Great for temp dialogue/ambience/SFX timing (or mute and finish audio in post).
Easy upgrade path||Keep the same prompt structure and rerun the keepers in LTX 2 for polish.
custom.specTitle: Real Specs – LTX 2 Fast in MaxVideoAI
custom.specNote: Specs reflect the live routing via Fal today.

specSections.title: Speed tests
specSections.intro: Designed for rapid iteration and volume. Use it to discover hooks and pacing before polishing.

specSections.title: Draft workflow
specSections.intro: The prompt format carries over to LTX 2, so upgrades are simple. Treat Fast as your draft layer.
items: Great for social drafts and loops.

custom.whatTitle: How LTX 2 Fast works in MaxVideoAI
custom.whatIntro1: Pick text or image mode, set 6–20 seconds, choose resolution and fps, then prompt. Audio is on by default and can be toggled off.
custom.whatIntro2: For 12–20s runs we automatically constrain to 1080p/25 fps to keep routes stable.
whatFlowSteps: Select LTX 2 Fast, choose Text → Video or Image → Video
Toggle audio on/off, add prompt and optional seed/negative
Upload one reference still for I2V
custom.galleryIntro: Recent LTX 2 Fast renders with audio on/off for social clips and quick iterations.
strengths: Draft-first workflow: generate a few variants, pick a winner, then rerun in Pro for polish.
One clear action + one camera move beats long “wish-list” prompts.
Audio works best with 1–2 clear cues (ambience + one key sound). Keep dialogue short.
boundaries: 12–20s runs are locked to 1080p / 25 fps in this routing.
Image→Video uses one reference image (no multi-image control).
safetyRules: No sexual content, and absolutely nothing involving minors.
No hateful, harassing, or violent/graphic content.
Don’t impersonate real people or public figures; use consent for any likeness/voice.
Don’t include private personal data (faces of non-consenting people, addresses, phone numbers, documents).
Keep outputs brand-safe; if you use dialogue, keep it clean and short.
demoPrompt: Tracking shot of a runner at golden hour on a boardwalk, camera follows from low angle, warm lens flare, gentle ocean ambience, audio on.
custom.multishotIntro1: Chain multiple 6–10s renders to build longer beats.
custom.multishotIntro2: Use seeds to keep look consistency across clips.
multishotTips: Keep one subject; reuse the same prompt backbone
Stitch in your editor; add VO/music there
custom.imageIntro: Upload a single sharp still and direct the motion and audio.
imageFlow: Set duration and fps; 12–20s lock to 1080p/25
Describe the camera move, pacing, and audio
imageWhy: Great for quick animatics from reference frames
comparisonPoints: Use Fast for rapid social clips; switch to LTX 2 for premium polish
For multi-aspect needs, pair with Wan or Veo; Fast is 16:9 only
For silent drafts, toggle audio off to reduce cost
custom.relatedSubtitle: Use LTX 2.3 Fast as the main current fast LTX page, or LTX 2.3 Pro when the workflow needs the broader current LTX surface.

relatedItems.title: LTX 2.3 Fast
relatedItems.description: Stay on a fast workflow but move to the current LTX 2.3 Fast routing for newer text-to-video and image-to-video generation.

relatedItems.title: LTX 2.3 Pro
relatedItems.description: Move up to LTX 2.3 Pro when the project needs audio-to-video, Extend, Retake, and the broader current-generation LTX workflow set.

custom.finalPara1: Ready to try LTX 2 Fast? Load your wallet and see the live price chip before you run.
custom.finalPara2: Swap to LTX 2 when you need maximum fidelity and synced audio.
custom.promptingTitle: How to Write a Great LTX 2 Fast Prompt
custom.promptingIntro: LTX 2 Fast prefers short prompts with one clear action and a single camera move.
custom.promptingTip: Tip: duration + fps are set in the UI - your prompt controls subject, action, camera, lighting, style, and optional sound cues.
promptingGlobalPrinciples: One subject, one action, one camera move.
Keep prompts short; avoid long lists.
custom.tipsIntro: LTX 2 Fast is most reliable when you treat it like a draft pass: simple shots, quick variations, then polish the winner.
promptingEngineWhy: Fast is optimized for quick iterations; simple prompts win.

promptingTabNotes.quick: Quick = variations. Use for fast iteration.
promptingTabNotes.structured: Structured = consistency. Use when you need reliable results.
promptingTabNotes.pro: Pro = continuity. Use for precise, repeatable looks.
promptingTabNotes.storyboard: Storyboard = beat timing. Use for mini-stories in one clip.

promptingTabs.label: Quick
promptingTabs.title: Quick prompt (fast iteration)
promptingTabs.description: Use 1–2 sentences when you want variations.
promptingTabs.copy:
[Subject + action] in [setting], [camera move], [lighting/style], [optional sound cue].
Negative: [text, logos, extra people, blur]

promptingTabs.label: Structured
promptingTabs.title: Structured prompt (best for reliable results)
promptingTabs.description: Separate information so the model can follow it consistently.
promptingTabs.copy:
Subject:
[Who/what + one action.]

Setting:
[Where + time + 2 anchors.]

Camera:
[Shot + angle + one move + lens feel.]

Look:
[Lighting + palette.]

Sound (optional):
[Ambience + 1 cue.]

Negative:
[Text, logos, extra people, blur.]

promptingTabs.label: Pro
promptingTabs.title: Pro prompt (ultra-specific "film crew brief")
promptingTabs.description: Use this when you need a very specific cinematic look or continuity across shots.
promptingTabs.copy:
Brief:
[Goal + vibe.]

Subject + wardrobe:
[Key traits + materials.]

Set:
[Location + anchors.]

Cinematography:
[Framing + angle + one move + lens.]

Lighting + grade:
[Key light + palette.]

Negative:
[What to avoid.]

promptingTabs.label: Storyboard
promptingTabs.title: Storyboard prompt (multi-shot / shot list)
promptingTabs.description: Use this when you want a mini-story in one clip. A storyboard prompt (aka multi-shot / shot list prompt) gives clear timing, camera direction, and continuity. Also called shot-list or sequenced prompts.
promptingTabs.copy:
Mini sequence (same subject + setting)
Beat 1 (start): [action + camera]
Beat 2 (middle): [action + camera]
Beat 3 (end): [final action + settle]

Look:
[Lighting + palette.]

Sound (optional):
[Ambience + 1-2 cues]

troubleshootingItems: Feels random / inconsistent → remove extra beats; rerun 2–3 tight variations.
Motion looks weird → slow the action and keep the camera move simple; reduce background clutter.
Subject drifts off-brand → start from a reference image; keep wardrobe, lighting, and palette consistent.
Text looks wrong → avoid readable signage, tiny UI, labels, and on-screen text.
Dialogue drifts → keep lines short; avoid long monologues.

hero.title: LTX 2 Fast
hero.subtitle: Fast 16:9 drafts up to 20s for text or image starts, audio-ready review passes, and 1080p to 4K checks.
hero.paragraph: Use LTX 2 Fast for the supported older fast LTX route when you need low-cost 16:9 drafts, longer 20s timing checks, image starts and high-res review options. Use LTX 2.3 Fast for the current fast LTX workflow.

features.title: Fast 16:9 drafts
features.body: Move quickly through older LTX timing and composition checks.

features.title: Text or image start
features.body: Use a prompt or one still to test product, subject or scene setup.

features.title: Up to 20s
features.body: Use longer 1080p/25 fps loops when pacing matters more than polish.

features.title: 1080p to 4K
features.body: Shorter checks can still use high-res review settings.

features.title: Audio-ready
features.body: Use the audio-enabled route for timing and review passes.

features.title: Pay-as-you-go
features.body: See exact live price before you generate.

decisionCards.title: LTX 2 Fast or LTX 2.3 Fast?
decisionCards.body: Use LTX 2 Fast for supported older 16:9 draft loops. Use LTX 2.3 Fast for the current fast LTX route and broader format coverage.

decisionCards.title: Need higher fidelity?
decisionCards.body: Move approved draft directions into LTX 2 or LTX 2.3 Pro when the shot needs a more polished pass.

decisionCards.title: Comparing fast value routes?
decisionCards.body: Compare LTX 2 Fast with Wan 2.5 when choosing between longer 16:9 LTX loops and lower-cost audio checks.

decisionCards.title: Need prompt examples?
decisionCards.body: Use short draft prompts with one motion beat, one camera move and a clear review goal.

referenceWorkflows.title: Draft prompt
referenceWorkflows.body: Keep one subject, one motion idea and one camera move so the fast route stays readable.

referenceWorkflows.title: Image start
referenceWorkflows.body: Use one still to lock the product, character or environment before testing motion.

referenceWorkflows.title: Long loop
referenceWorkflows.body: Use 12-20s only for 1080p timing checks where pacing matters more than detail.

referenceWorkflows.title: High-res short check
referenceWorkflows.body: Use 1440p or 4K on shorter clips when you need to inspect detail.

referenceWorkflows.title: Upgrade path
referenceWorkflows.body: Move winning directions into LTX 2.3 Fast or Pro depending on the current workflow needed.

pricingCopy.title: LTX 2 Fast draft pricing
pricingCopy.subtitle: Preset draft totals - see the exact live price in the app before you generate.
pricingCopy.footnote: All prices are MaxVideoAI display prices in USD credits for preset scenarios.

meta.title: LTX 2 Fast: Pricing, 20s Drafts & Examples | MaxVideoAI
meta.description: Use LTX 2 Fast for supported older LTX 16:9 drafts, 20s timing checks, text-to-video, image-to-video, high-res review options and pricing.

section.title: How to Write a Great LTX 2 Fast Prompt
section.intro: LTX 2 Fast prefers short prompts with one clear action and a single camera move.
section.tip: Tip: duration + fps are set in the UI - your prompt controls subject, action, camera, lighting, style, and optional sound cues.

tabNotes.body: Quick = variations. Use for fast iteration.
Structured = consistency. Use when you need reliable results.
Pro = continuity. Use for precise, repeatable looks.
Storyboard = beat timing. Use for mini-stories in one clip.

globalPrinciples: One subject, one action, one camera move.
Keep prompts short; avoid long lists.
engineWhy: Fast is optimized for quick iterations; simple prompts win.

demo.title: Demo prompt – fast social clip
demo.prompt:
Subject: Young woman at a busy crosswalk
Action: Waits, then crosses in golden-hour light
Camera: Starts behind her, then moves into a smooth handheld follow
Style: Busy city, warm light, cinematic social clip
Audio: City ambience, no dialogue

summary.action: Waits, then crosses in golden-hour light
summary.camera: Starts behind her, then moves into a smooth handheld follow
